reply:
[[BLOCK:slide12_shape1]]
Too many assumptions are made regarding the Residuals (Residual) and the predictors.  
The model tends to overfit the data.  
It becomes unstable when some predictors are strongly correlated, a situation known as Multi-collinearity (Multi-collinearity).  
There is no unique solution when dealing with a large dataset.  
For sample size, the conventional rule of thumb is 1 predictor to 10 observations; it is not feasible to run OLS Regression if the number of predictors (p) exceeds the number of observations (n).  
The model must adhere to linearity.
